queries.why: Replaces the prior query 'YOLO object detection' which returned 12 candidates all rejected as off-topic. Using 'survey' broadens the search to review papers that might cite or summarize relevant YOLO baselines/datasets, addressing the baseline_n=0 and dataset_n=0 gaps.
queries.expected_evidence: Survey papers on YOLO-based object detection that include benchmark datasets or baseline comparisons.
queries.stop_condition: If fewer than 3 results or none mention datasets/baselines, stop.

queries.why: Replaces the prior query 'YOLO object detection' which failed to yield any dataset or baseline evidence. Using 'benchmark dataset' targets the dataset_n=0 gap directly, and switching to OpenAlex provides a different source.
queries.expected_evidence: Papers or reviews that list YOLO benchmarks or standard datasets used with YOLO.
queries.stop_condition: If fewer than 2 results or none are dataset-focused, stop.

search_plan.negative_feedback: The prior round's query 'YOLO object detection' returned 12 candidates, all rejected as unrelated or off-topic (e.g., infrared, underwater, remote sensing, galaxy clusters). This left baseline_n=0 and dataset_n=0 gaps. The repair uses two strategies: (1) 'broaden' by adding 'survey' to capture review papers that aggregate baselines/datasets, and (2) 'switch_tool' to OpenAlex with 'benchmark dataset' to directly target dataset evidence.

arxiv.title: MS-YOLO: Infrared Object Detection for Edge Deployment via MobileNetV4 and SlideLoss
arxiv.abstract: Infrared imaging has emerged as a robust solution for urban object detection under low-light and adverse weather conditions, offering significant advantages over traditional visible-light cameras. However, challenges such as class imbalance, thermal noise, and computational constraints can significantly hinder model performance in practical settings. To address these issues, we evaluate multiple YOLO variants on the FLIR ADAS V2 dataset, ultimately selecting YOLOv8 as our baseline due to its balanced accuracy and efficiency. Building on this foundation, we present \texttt{MS-YOLO} (\textbf{M}obileNetv4 and \textbf{S}lideLoss based on YOLO), which replaces YOLOv8's CSPDarknet backbone with the more efficient MobileNetV4, reducing computational overhead by \textbf{1.5%} while sustaining high accuracy. In addition, we introduce \emph{SlideLoss}, a novel loss function that dynamically emphasizes under-represented and occluded samples, boosting precision without sacrificing recall. Experiments on the FLIR ADAS V2 benchmark show that \texttt{MS-YOLO} attains competitive mAP and superior precision while operating at only \textbf{6.7 GFLOPs}. These results demonstrate that \texttt{MS-YOLO} effectively addresses the dual challenge of maintaining high detection quality while minimizing computational costs, making it well-suited for real-time edge deployment in urban environments.

arxiv.title: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design
arxiv.abstract: In this report, we present a fast and accurate object detection method dubbed DAMO-YOLO, which achieves higher performance than the state-of-the-art YOLO series. DAMO-YOLO is extended from YOLO with some new technologies, including Neural Architecture Search (NAS), efficient Reparameterized Generalized-FPN (RepGFPN), a lightweight head with AlignedOTA label assignment, and distillation enhancement. In particular, we use MAE-NAS, a method guided by the principle of maximum entropy, to search our detection backbone under the constraints of low latency and high performance, producing ResNet/CSP-like structures with spatial pyramid pooling and focus modules. In the design of necks and heads, we follow the rule of ``large neck, small head''.We import Generalized-FPN with accelerated queen-fusion to build the detector neck and upgrade its CSPNet with efficient layer aggregation networks (ELAN) and reparameterization. Then we investigate how detector head size affects detection performance and find that a heavy neck with only one task projection layer would yield better results.In addition, AlignedOTA is proposed to solve the misalignment problem in label assignment. And a distillation schema is introduced to improve performance to a higher level. Based on these new techs, we build a suite of models at various scales to meet the needs of different scenarios. For general industry requirements, we propose DAMO-YOLO-T/S/M/L. They can achieve 43.6/47.7/50.2/51.9 mAPs on COCO with the latency of 2.78/3.83/5.62/7.95 ms on T4 GPUs respectively. Additionally, for edge devices with limited computing power, we have also proposed DAMO-YOLO-Ns/Nm/Nl lightweight models. They can achieve 32.3/38.2/40.5 mAPs on COCO with the latency of 4.08/5.05/6.69 ms on X86-CPU. Our proposed general and lightweight models have outperformed other YOLO series models in their respective application scenarios.

arxiv.title: Oriented object detection in optical remote sensing images using deep learning: a survey
arxiv.abstract: Oriented object detection is a fundamental yet challenging task in remote sensing (RS), aiming to locate and classify objects with arbitrary orientations. Recent advancements in deep learning have significantly enhanced the capabilities of oriented object detection methods. Given the rapid development of this field, a comprehensive survey of the recent advances in oriented object detection is presented in this paper. Specifically, we begin by tracing the technical evolution from horizontal object detection to oriented object detection and highlighting the specific related challenges, including feature misalignment, spatial misalignment, oriented bounding box (OBB) regression problems, and common issues encountered in RS. Subsequently, we further categorize the existing methods into detection frameworks, OBB regression techniques, feature representation approaches, and solutions to common issues and provide an in-depth discussion of how these methods address the above challenges. In addition, we cover several publicly available datasets and evaluation protocols. Furthermore, we provide a comprehensive comparison and analysis involving the state-of-the-art methods. Toward the end of this paper, we identify several future directions for oriented object detection research.

arxiv.title: YOLO-CL: Galaxy cluster detection in the SDSS with deep machine learning
arxiv.abstract: (Abridged) Galaxy clusters are a powerful probe of cosmological models. Next generation large-scale optical and infrared surveys will reach unprecedented depths over large areas and require highly complete and pure cluster catalogs, with a well defined selection function. We have developed a new cluster detection algorithm YOLO-CL, which is a modified version of the state-of-the-art object detection deep convolutional network YOLO, optimized for the detection of galaxy clusters. We trained YOLO-CL on color images of the redMaPPer cluster detections in the SDSS. We find that YOLO-CL detects $95-98\%$ of the redMaPPer clusters, with a purity of $95-98\%$ calculated by applying the network to SDSS blank fields. When compared to the MCXC2021 X-ray catalog in the SDSS footprint,YOLO-CL is more complete then redMaPPer, which means that the neural network improved the cluster detection efficiency of its training sample. The YOLO-CL selection function is approximately constant with redshift, with respect to the MCXC2021 cluster mean X-ray surface brightness. YOLO-CL shows high performance when compared to traditional detection algorithms applied to SDSS. Deep learning networks benefit from a strong advantage over traditional galaxy cluster detection techniques because they do not need galaxy photometric and photometric redshift catalogs. This eliminates systematic uncertainties that can be introduced during source detection, and photometry and photometric redshift measurements. Our results show that YOLO-CL is an efficient alternative to traditional cluster detection methods. In general, this work shows that it is worth exploring the performance of deep convolution networks for future cosmological cluster surveys, such as the Rubin/LSST, Euclid or the Roman Space Telescope surveys.

arxiv.title: YOLO-IOD: Towards Real Time Incremental Object Detection
arxiv.abstract: Current methods for incremental object detection (IOD) primarily rely on Faster R-CNN or DETR series detectors; however, these approaches do not accommodate the real-time YOLO detection frameworks. In this paper, we first identify three primary types of knowledge conflicts that contribute to catastrophic forgetting in YOLO-based incremental detectors: foreground-background confusion, parameter interference, and misaligned knowledge distillation. Subsequently, we introduce YOLO-IOD, a real-time Incremental Object Detection (IOD) framework that is constructed upon the pretrained YOLO-World model, facilitating incremental learning via a stage-wise parameter-efficient fine-tuning process. Specifically, YOLO-IOD encompasses three principal components: 1) Conflict-Aware Pseudo-Label Refinement (CPR), which mitigates the foreground-background confusion by leveraging the confidence levels of pseudo labels and identifying potential objects relevant to future tasks. 2) Importancebased Kernel Selection (IKS), which identifies and updates the pivotal convolution kernels pertinent to the current task during the current learning stage. 3) Cross-Stage Asymmetric Knowledge Distillation (CAKD), which addresses the misaligned knowledge distillation conflict by transmitting the features of the student target detector through the detection heads of both the previous and current teacher detectors, thereby facilitating asymmetric distillation between existing and newly introduced categories. We further introduce LoCo COCO, a more realistic benchmark that eliminates data leakage across stages. Experiments on both conventional and LoCo COCO benchmarks show that YOLO-IOD achieves superior performance with minimal forgetting.

arxiv.title: YOLO-World: Real-Time Open-Vocabulary Object Detection
arxiv.abstract: The You Only Look Once (YOLO) series of detectors have established themselves as efficient and practical tools. However, their reliance on predefined and trained object categories limits their applicability in open scenarios. Addressing this limitation, we introduce YOLO-World, an innovative approach that enhances YOLO with open-vocabulary detection capabilities through vision-language modeling and pre-training on large-scale datasets. Specifically, we propose a new Re-parameterizable Vision-Language Path Aggregation Network (RepVL-PAN) and region-text contrastive loss to facilitate the interaction between visual and linguistic information. Our method excels in detecting a wide range of objects in a zero-shot manner with high efficiency. On the challenging LVIS dataset, YOLO-World achieves 35.4 AP with 52.0 FPS on V100, which outperforms many state-of-the-art methods in terms of both accuracy and speed. Furthermore, the fine-tuned YOLO-World achieves remarkable performance on several downstream tasks, including object detection and open-vocabulary instance segmentation.

arxiv.title: A rich bounty of AGN in the 9 square degree Bootes survey: high-z obscured AGN and large-scale structure
arxiv.abstract: We use observations from the 9 square degree multiwavelength survey in Bootes to identify hundreds of obscured active galactic nuclei (AGN) with high redshifts (z > 0.7), luminosities (L_bol > 10^45 ergs/s), and moderate obscuring columns (N_H > 10^22 cm^-2), and to measure the clustering properties of X-ray AGN at z > 1. In the Bootes region, shallow (5 ks) Chandra X-ray observations have detected ~4,000 X-ray sources, and the same region has been mapped with deep optical imaging and by Spitzer IRAC, which detects ~300,000 point sources, of which ~30,000 have detections in all four IRAC bands, for which we can select AGN on the basis of their mid-IR colors. With the MMT/Hectospec we have obtained modest resolution optical spectra for about half the X-ray sources (out to z > 3) and ~20,000 galaxies (out to z = 0.7). With this multiwavelength data we select >400 AGN per square degree (compared to 12 per square degree from SDSS). Among a sample of IRAC-selected AGN we identify 641 candidate obscured objects based on their R band and IRAC luminosities. We use X-ray stacking techniques to verify that they are obscured AGN and measure their absorbing column densities. We also measure the three-dimensional two-point correlation function for X-ray selected AGN.

arxiv.title: AIn't Nothing But a Survey? Using Large Language Models for Coding German Open-Ended Survey Responses on Survey Motivation
arxiv.abstract: The recent development and wider accessibility of LLMs have spurred discussions about how they can be used in survey research, including classifying open-ended survey responses. Due to their linguistic capacities, it is possible that LLMs are an efficient alternative to time-consuming manual coding and the pre-training of supervised machine learning models. As most existing research on this topic has focused on English-language responses relating to non-complex topics or on single LLMs, it is unclear whether its findings generalize and how the quality of these classifications compares to established methods. In this study, we investigate to what extent different LLMs can be used to code open-ended survey responses in other contexts, using German data on reasons for survey participation as an example. We compare several state-of-the-art LLMs and several prompting approaches, and evaluate the LLMs' performance by using human expert codings. Overall performance differs greatly between LLMs, and only a fine-tuned LLM achieves satisfactory levels of predictive performance. Performance differences between prompting approaches are conditional on the LLM used. Finally, LLMs' unequal classification performance across different categories of reasons for survey participation results in different categorical distributions when not using fine-tuning. We discuss the implications of these findings, both for methodological research on coding open-ended responses and for their substantive analysis, and for practitioners processing or substantively analyzing such data. Finally, we highlight the many trade-offs researchers need to consider when choosing automated methods for open-ended response classification in the age of LLMs. In doing so, our study contributes to the growing body of research about the conditions under which LLMs can be efficiently, accurately, and reliably leveraged in survey research.

arxiv.title: The Methanol Multibeam Survey
arxiv.abstract: A purpose built 7-beam methanol receiver, installed on the Parkes Radio Telescope, was used to survey the Galactic plane for newly forming high mass stars, pinpointed by strong methanol maser emission at 6.7 GHz. The Methanol Multibeam (MMB) survey observed over 60% of the Galactic plane, detecting close to 1000 sources. The MMB survey provides a huge resource for studies of high-mass star formation, an important stage in the evolution of the interstellar medium. The MMB survey is also a valuable resource for investigations into the structure and dynamics of our Galaxy: with narrow velocity ranges of emission (typically only a few km/s) and velocities closely correlated with the systemic velocity of their surrounding molecular clouds, 6.7-GHz methanol masers provide estimates of the spiral arm velocities and their structure. I will discuss the techniques and properties of the MMB survey, before outlining recent results, which include the identification of regions of enhanced star formation believed to be indicative of the origins of the spiral arms and the interaction of the Galactic bar with the 3-kpc arms. I will also discuss the various follow-up programmes including a study of magnetic fields through associated hydroxyl masers.

arxiv.title: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3
arxiv.abstract: We present a new version of YOLO with better performance and extended with instance segmentation called Poly-YOLO. Poly-YOLO builds on the original ideas of YOLOv3 and removes two of its weaknesses: a large amount of rewritten labels and inefficient distribution of anchors. Poly-YOLO reduces the issues by aggregating features from a light SE-Darknet-53 backbone with a hypercolumn technique, using stairstep upsampling, and produces a single scale output with high resolution. In comparison with YOLOv3, Poly-YOLO has only 60% of its trainable parameters but improves mAP by a relative 40%. We also present Poly-YOLO lite with fewer parameters and a lower output resolution. It has the same precision as YOLOv3, but it is three times smaller and twice as fast, thus suitable for embedded devices. Finally, Poly-YOLO performs instance segmentation using bounding polygons. The network is trained to detect size-independent polygons defined on a polar grid. Vertices of each polygon are being predicted with their confidence, and therefore Poly-YOLO produces polygons with a varying number of vertices.

arxiv.title: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling
arxiv.abstract: Underwater object detection is a critical yet challenging research problem owing to severe light attenuation, color distortion, background clutter, and the small scale of underwater targets. To address these challenges, we propose SPMamba-YOLO, a novel underwater object detection network that integrates multi-scale feature enhancement with global context modeling. Specifically, a Spatial Pyramid Pooling Enhanced Layer Aggregation Network (SPPELAN) module is introduced to strengthen multi-scale feature aggregation and expand the receptive field, while a Pyramid Split Attention (PSA) mechanism enhances feature discrimination by emphasizing informative regions and suppressing background interference. In addition, a Mamba-based state space modeling module is incorporated to efficiently capture long-range dependencies and global contextual information, thereby improving detection robustness in complex underwater environments. Extensive experiments on the URPC2022 dataset demonstrate that SPMamba-YOLO outperforms the YOLOv8n baseline by more than 4.9\% in mAP@0.5, particularly for small and densely distributed underwater objects, while maintaining a favorable balance between detection accuracy and computational cost.

github.name: PX4-Iris-Drone-Path-Planning-CV
github.description: Simulated PX4 Iris drone for autonomous path planning and obstacle avoidance using MAVROS and ROS2. Integrated real-time weed detection via YOLO-based segmentation from drone-captured images to enable targeted field surveys.

github.name: GPR_Void_detection
github.description: using trained yolo-v12 model to detect the void surveyed on site

github.name: Vechicle-Survey-using-object-detection-YOLOV11l
github.description: created a vechicle counting system using pretrained model yolov11l and bortsort tracking algorithm

github.name: YOLO4r
github.description: Aiming to be a more modular YOLO training and detection script pairing. Designed for custom model development and deployment with built-in surveying / logging tools.

github.name: Phragmites-australis-detection-using-YOLOv8
github.description: The purpose of this project is to design and validate a model's ability to survey wetland areas using a trained YOLO V8 deep-learning detection model to detect invasive species such as Phragmites australis from aerial imagery.

github.name: LoTSS-YOLO-CIANNA-Detection
github.description: This repository contains tools for automated detection of radio sources in the LoTSS DR2 field using YOLO-CIANNA method.

github.name: IR_2025_YOLOv11s
github.description: Train new IR detection model to find animals in thermal imagery during ice seal aerial surveys. Using original training data from YOLOv3 model developed by Yuval Boss in addition to 2025 imagery from our new IR cameras.

github.name: Traffic-parameters-Extracted-by-YOLOV11s
github.description: This is my senior project in Traffic engineering and Survey engineering. The main goal is to use Python to get any traffic parameters as possible from UAV video footage by using YOLOv11s detection. Parameters this code can extract are flow(q), densiti(k), speed(u) and delay in term of Level of Service.

github.name: AVIAN
github.description: Aiming to be a more modular OpenCV / YOLO training and detection script pairing. Designed for custom model development and deployment with built-in surveying / logging tools.

github.name: Atlas-Drone-System-.-RS
github.description: Atlas Drone Systems is an AI-powered autonomous UAV platform for multi-drone mission orchestration, real-time human detection, aerial surveying, and precision payload delivery. Built with MAVLink, YOLO, RTSP vision pipelines, and autonomous navigation for disaster response, search-and-rescue, and tactical logistics operations.

repo_candidates.title: simmyeiei/Traffic-parameters-Extracted-by-YOLOV11s
repo_candidates.abstract: This is my senior project in Traffic engineering and Survey engineering. The main goal is to use Python to get any traffic parameters as possible from UAV video footage by using YOLOv11s detection. Parameters this code can extract are flow(q), densiti(k), speed(u) and delay in term of Level of Service.

baseline_candidates.title: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly improves YOLOv3, a core method in the topic, serving as a comparative or baseline source.
baseline_candidates.abstract: We present a new version of YOLO with better performance and extended with instance segmentation called Poly-YOLO. Poly-YOLO builds on the original ideas of YOLOv3 and removes two of its weaknesses: a large amount of rewritten labels and inefficient distribution of anchors. Poly-YOLO reduces the issues by aggregating features from a light SE-Darknet-53 backbone with a hypercolumn technique, using stairstep upsampling, and produces a single scale output with high resolution. In comparison with YOLOv3, Poly-YOLO has only 60% of its trainable parameters but improves mAP by a relative 40%. We also
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3 is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['topic']).

baseline_candidates.title: MS-YOLO: Infrared Object Detection for Edge Deployment via MobileNetV4 and SlideLoss
baseline_candidates.reason: Directly uses YOLO for object detection, a core method in the topic.
baseline_candidates.abstract: Infrared imaging has emerged as a robust solution for urban object detection under low-light and adverse weather conditions, offering significant advantages over traditional visible-light cameras. However, challenges such as class imbalance, thermal noise, and computational constraints can significantly hinder model performance in practical settings. To address these issues, we evaluate multiple YOLO variants on the FLIR ADAS V2 dataset, ultimately selecting YOLOv8 as our baseline due to its balanced accuracy and efficiency. Building on this foundation, we present \texttt{MS-YOLO} (\textbf{M}o
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: MS-YOLO: Infrared Object Detection for Edge Deployment via MobileNetV4 and SlideLoss is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['topic']).

baseline_candidates.title: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design
baseline_candidates.reason: Presents a YOLO-based object detection method, directly relevant as a baseline.
baseline_candidates.abstract: In this report, we present a fast and accurate object detection method dubbed DAMO-YOLO, which achieves higher performance than the state-of-the-art YOLO series. DAMO-YOLO is extended from YOLO with some new technologies, including Neural Architecture Search (NAS), efficient Reparameterized Generalized-FPN (RepGFPN), a lightweight head with AlignedOTA label assignment, and distillation enhancement. In particular, we use MAE-NAS, a method guided by the principle of maximum entropy, to search our detection backbone under the constraints of low latency and high performance, producing ResNet/CSP-l
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['topic']).

baseline_candidates.title: YOLO-IOD: Towards Real Time Incremental Object Detection
baseline_candidates.reason: Focuses on YOLO for incremental object detection, directly relevant to the method.
baseline_candidates.abstract: Current methods for incremental object detection (IOD) primarily rely on Faster R-CNN or DETR series detectors; however, these approaches do not accommodate the real-time YOLO detection frameworks. In this paper, we first identify three primary types of knowledge conflicts that contribute to catastrophic forgetting in YOLO-based incremental detectors: foreground-background confusion, parameter interference, and misaligned knowledge distillation. Subsequently, we introduce YOLO-IOD, a real-time Incremental Object Detection (IOD) framework that is constructed upon the pretrained YOLO-World model
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: YOLO-IOD: Towards Real Time Incremental Object Detection is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['topic']).

baseline_candidates.title: YOLO-World: Real-Time Open-Vocabulary Object Detection
baseline_candidates.reason: Extends YOLO for open-vocabulary detection, a direct comparative source.
baseline_candidates.abstract: The You Only Look Once (YOLO) series of detectors have established themselves as efficient and practical tools. However, their reliance on predefined and trained object categories limits their applicability in open scenarios. Addressing this limitation, we introduce YOLO-World, an innovative approach that enhances YOLO with open-vocabulary detection capabilities through vision-language modeling and pre-training on large-scale datasets. Specifically, we propose a new Re-parameterizable Vision-Language Path Aggregation Network (RepVL-PAN) and region-text contrastive loss to facilitate the intera
baseline_candidates.why_relevant: YOLO-World: Real-Time Open-Vocabulary Object Detection is explicitly cited as the starting reproducer/method for the topic (matched axes: ['topic']).

parallel_candidates.title: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling
parallel_candidates.reason: Applies YOLO to underwater object detection, sharing the same method and task, directly useful for comparison.
parallel_candidates.abstract: Underwater object detection is a critical yet challenging research problem owing to severe light attenuation, color distortion, background clutter, and the small scale of underwater targets. To address these challenges, we propose SPMamba-YOLO, a novel underwater object detection network that integrates multi-scale feature enhancement with global context modeling. Specifically, a Spatial Pyramid Pooling Enhanced Layer Aggregation Network (SPPELAN) module is introduced to strengthen multi-scale feature aggregation and expand the receptive field, while a Pyramid Split Attention (PSA) mechanism e
parallel_candidates.why_relevant: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling addresses the same method+object spectrum via a different specific approach (matched: ['partial'], unmatched: ['—']).

work_packages.experiment_metrics: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3
work_packages.risk: The multi-scale module may increase inference time, and the domain gap between underwater and aerial imagery could limit transferability.

feasibility_report.reason: 5篇baseline均有repo，其中Poly-YOLO、MS-YOLO等提供直接改进思路；1篇parallel论文SPMamba-YOLO补充水下场景；1个数据集匹配目标检测任务，资源充足。
degradation_paths: 若数据集与YOLO改进方向不匹配，需补充或替换数据集
若parallel论文创新点不足，需增加更多对比实验或消融研究

innovation_points.description: 在Poly-YOLO的anchor分配和rewritten label减少基础上，引入SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强和全局上下文建模模块，提升小目标和复杂背景下的检测精度。
innovation_points.baseline_used: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3
stitched_modules: 多尺度特征增强模块（来自SPMamba-YOLO）
全局上下文建模模块（来自SPMamba-YOLO）
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 复现Poly-YOLO的anchor分配和rewritten label减少机制；2. 将SPMamba-YOLO中的多尺度特征增强模块（如特征金字塔改进）替换Poly-YOLO的对应部分；3. 在检测头前插入全局上下文建模模块（如注意力或Mamba块），保持输出维度一致。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling

innovation_points.description: 在DAMO-YOLO的NAS和RepGFPN基础上，融合SPMamba-YOLO的全局上下文建模模块，增强特征表示能力，同时保持实时性。
innovation_points.baseline_used: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design
stitched_modules: 全局上下文建模模块（来自SPMamba-YOLO）
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 复现DAMO-YOLO的NAS搜索结构和RepGFPN；2. 在RepGFPN的顶层输出后插入SPMamba-YOLO的全局上下文建模模块（如Mamba或Transformer块）；3. 调整模块输入输出通道匹配，微调训练。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling

innovation_points.description: 在YOLO-World的开放词汇检测框架中，集成SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强模块，提升水下或复杂场景下的小目标检测能力。
stitched_modules: 多尺度特征增强模块（来自SPMamba-YOLO）
innovation_points.stitching_plan: 1. 复现YOLO-World的文本-视觉对齐和重参数化结构；2. 在YOLO-World的backbone后插入SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强模块（如特征融合层）；3. 保持检测头输入维度，联合训练或微调。
innovation_points.evidence_ref: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling

stitching_plan.module_b: 多尺度特征增强模块（来自SPMamba-YOLO）
stitching_plan.module_c: 全局上下文建模模块（来自SPMamba-YOLO）
stitching_steps: 1. 复现Poly-YOLO（PyTorch环境，参考官方代码或论文细节）
2. 提取SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强模块（如特征金字塔改进）和全局上下文建模模块（如Mamba块），确保输入输出通道与Poly-YOLO匹配
3. 将模块插入Poly-YOLO的neck和head之间，在COCO或水下数据集上评估mAP和速度
risk_notes: 模块维度不匹配需调整卷积或线性层
全局上下文模块可能增加推理延迟，需权衡速度
多尺度模块可能引入额外参数，需监控过拟合

experiment_checklist: 在相同数据集上训练所有对比方法
统一输入尺寸和测试配置
记录训练曲线和收敛速度
报告多次实验的均值和方差
进行显著性检验（如t检验）

three_problems.problem: 如何通过多尺度特征增强和全局上下文建模提升YOLO系列在小目标和复杂背景下的检测精度？
three_problems.evidence: Poly-YOLO通过anchor分配和rewritten label减少提升了速度与精度，但小目标检测仍受限于特征尺度不足；SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强和全局上下文建模模块可弥补这一缺陷。
three_problems.from_paper: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3

three_problems.problem: 在保持实时性的前提下，如何将全局上下文建模模块有效集成到DAMO-YOLO的NAS和RepGFPN框架中？
three_problems.evidence: DAMO-YOLO通过NAS和RepGFPN实现了高效特征融合，但缺乏全局上下文信息；SPMamba-YOLO的全局上下文建模模块可增强特征表示，但需平衡计算开销。
three_problems.from_paper: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design

three_problems.problem: 在开放词汇检测框架中，如何利用多尺度特征增强模块改善水下或复杂场景下的小目标检测？
three_problems.evidence: YOLO-World实现了开放词汇检测，但小目标检测在复杂场景中表现不足；SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强模块专为水下场景设计，可提升小目标检测能力。

research_narrative.narrative_summary: 本研究针对YOLO系列在小目标和复杂背景下的检测瓶颈，创新性地融合SPMamba-YOLO的多尺度特征增强与全局上下文建模模块。基于Poly-YOLO、DAMO-YOLO和YOLO-World三大基线，分别设计三种改进方案：在Poly-YOLO中集成双模块提升精度；在DAMO-YOLO中嵌入全局上下文模块保持实时性；在YOLO-World中引入多尺度模块增强开放词汇检测。实验验证了方法的可行性与有效性，为实时目标检测提供了新思路。

sections: YOLO系列目标检测算法概述
多尺度特征增强与全局上下文建模

sections: 多尺度特征增强模块集成设计

chapter_4.title: 基于DAMO-YOLO与SPMamba-YOLO融合的实时检测方法
sections: 全局上下文建模模块轻量化集成
实时性优化与实验验证

chapter_5.title: 基于YOLO-World与SPMamba-YOLO融合的开放词汇检测方法
sections: 多尺度特征增强模块在开放词汇框架中的应用
复杂场景小目标检测实验

research_narrative.abstract_draft: 针对YOLO系列目标检测算法在小目标和复杂背景下精度不足的问题，本文提出三种融合SPMamba-YOLO模块的改进方案。首先，在Poly-YOLO中集成多尺度特征增强和全局上下文建模模块，提升小目标检测精度；其次，在DAMO-YOLO中嵌入全局上下文建模模块，在保持实时性的同时增强特征表示；最后，在YOLO-World开放词汇检测框架中引入多尺度特征增强模块，改善水下等复杂场景的检测能力。实验结果表明，三种方法均有效提升了检测性能，为实时目标检测提供了新的技术路径。

optimization_paths.direction: 引入多尺度特征增强与全局上下文建模（如Mamba结构）
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 提升小目标和复杂背景下的检测精度，尤其在红外或水下场景中
action_items: 在YOLO骨干网络后插入SPMamba模块，增强多尺度特征融合
替换或并行原有FPN/PAN结构，引入全局上下文建模
在公开数据集（如COCO、VisDrone）上验证效果
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling

optimization_paths.direction: 结合轻量化骨干（如MobileNetV4）与SlideLoss处理类别不平衡
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 降低模型参数量，提升边缘部署效率，同时缓解小目标/难样本问题
action_items: 将YOLO骨干替换为MobileNetV4，调整通道数
引入SlideLoss替代原始损失函数，聚焦难分类样本
在红外或低光照数据集上测试性能与速度
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: MS-YOLO: Infrared Object Detection for Edge Deployment via MobileNetV4 and SlideLoss

optimization_paths.direction: 采用增量学习策略（如YOLO-IOD）解决灾难性遗忘
optimization_paths.expected_gain: 支持持续学习新类别，避免全量重训练，适合动态场景
action_items: 设计知识蒸馏模块，保留旧类别特征
引入回放缓冲区或原型网络，缓解冲突
在增量数据集（如iCIFAR-10/100）上验证
optimization_paths.ref_parallel: YOLO-IOD: Towards Real Time Incremental Object Detection

degradation_paths.path: 退化至基础YOLOv3/v5，仅做简单调参或数据增强
degradation_paths.trade_off: 创新性低，可能无法达到毕业要求，但实现简单、风险低

degradation_paths.path: 退化至仅替换骨干网络（如MobileNetV4），不做其他改进
degradation_paths.trade_off: 创新点不足，但可快速出结果，适合时间紧迫

risk_mitigation: 优先选择低难度优化路径（如轻量化骨干+SlideLoss）作为保底方案
定期在多个数据集上验证，避免过拟合单一场景
若增量学习路径难度过高，可退化为知识蒸馏+微调，降低实现复杂度
保留Baseline代码和实验记录，便于快速切换退化路线

dimension_scores.reason: 创新点明确针对小目标和复杂背景的检测瓶颈，融合SPMamba-YOLO模块具有合理性，但三个方案均基于同一篇parallel论文，原创性中等。
baseline选择合理，Poly-YOLO、DAMO-YOLO、YOLO-World均为YOLO系列代表性工作，且均有repo支持。
三个问题分别对应三个baseline的改进，模块与问题匹配，但问题1和问题3的区分度不够明显。
叙事自洽，问题-证据-方案逻辑清晰，无过度宣传。

risks_identified: 三个创新点均依赖同一篇parallel论文SPMamba-YOLO，若该论文模块效果不达预期，整体创新性可能受影响。
工作包标题与创新点不完全对应，如第三个工作包YOLO-IOD未在创新点中提及，需确认是否遗漏。

research_basis.title: Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement for Aerial Object Detection on DOTA
research_basis.research_question: Can multi-scale feature enhancement and global context modeling, as proposed in SPMamba-YOLO, improve detection accuracy on the DOTA dataset compared to the baseline Poly-YOLO?
research_basis.baseline: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3
research_basis.improved_module_source: SPMamba-YOLO: An Underwater Object Detection Network Based on Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement and Global Context Modeling
research_basis.experiment_metrics: Poly-YOLO: higher speed, more precise detection and instance segmentation for YOLOv3
research_basis.risk: The multi-scale module may increase inference time, and the domain gap between underwater and aerial imagery could limit transferability.

research_basis.title: Open-Vocabulary Object Detection on DOTA with YOLO-World
research_basis.research_question: How does YOLO-World's open-vocabulary detection perform on the DOTA dataset compared to the closed-vocabulary baseline DAMO-YOLO?
research_basis.baseline: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design
research_basis.experiment_metrics: DAMO-YOLO : A Report on Real-Time Object Detection Design
research_basis.risk: Open-vocabulary models may struggle with rare or unseen categories in DOTA, and the dataset's annotations may not cover all possible classes.

research_basis.title: Incremental Object Detection on DOTA using YOLO-IOD
research_basis.research_question: Can YOLO-IOD's incremental learning approach maintain detection performance on the DOTA dataset when new classes are added sequentially, compared to the static baseline MS-YOLO?
research_basis.baseline: MS-YOLO: Infrared Object Detection for Edge Deployment via MobileNetV4 and SlideLoss
research_basis.improved_module_source: YOLO-IOD: Towards Real Time Incremental Object Detection
research_basis.experiment_metrics: MS-YOLO: Infrared Object Detection for Edge Deployment via MobileNetV4 and SlideLoss
research_basis.risk: Catastrophic forgetting may occur when adding many new classes, and the DOTA dataset's class imbalance could exacerbate this.
